guides.title: Optimize how you ship UI in Quadcode
guides.desc: The full playbook: brief → screen map → component system → states → polish. Cut rework and get production-ready interfaces faster.

steps.title: Lock the product brief in one pass
steps.text: Before any pixels, force a tight brief: who it's for, the one job of the screen, and what 'done' looks like. Ambiguous briefs are what burn tokens later.
steps.prompt: Act as a product designer inside Quadcode. Turn my rough idea into a locked UI brief with: (1) user + job-to-be-done, (2) primary screen goal in one sentence, (3) must-have vs nice-to-have, (4) platform (web/desktop/mobile) + density, (5) success criteria I can check visually. Ask at most 3 clarifying questions only if a blocker is missing — otherwise assume sensible defaults and proceed.

steps.title: Map screens before you generate
steps.text: List every screen and the navigation between them. Agents that jump straight to a pretty mock invent dead ends; a map keeps the system coherent.
steps.prompt: From the locked brief, produce a screen map: numbered screens, purpose of each, entry/exit, and the primary CTA. Output as a compact table + a one-line user flow. Flag any screen that can be merged or cut. Do not write visual styles yet.

steps.title: Define the component system once
steps.text: Tokens and components first — then every screen reuses them. This is the biggest speed win: change the system once instead of restyling ten mocks.
steps.prompt: Design a minimal UI system for this product: color tokens (bg/surface/text/accent/danger), type scale, spacing scale (4/8/12/16/24), radius, elevation, and a component inventory (Button, Input, Select, Card, Nav, Table/List, Modal, Toast, Empty state). Specify default / hover / focus / disabled for interactive parts. Keep it implementable in HTML/CSS or React without a design-tool roundtrip.

steps.title: Build the hero screen with real states
steps.text: Generate the main screen in three states: empty, loading, populated. Interfaces fail in the edges — bake them in on the first pass.
steps.prompt: Using the system above, implement the primary screen as production-ready UI (HTML/CSS or the project's stack). Include: empty state, loading skeleton, and populated data. Use realistic copy and sample data — no lorem. Match the tokens exactly. Prefer one composition, strong hierarchy, no decorative card clutter.

steps.title: Stress-test and ship the checklist
steps.text: Close with a QA pass the agent can run itself: contrast, keyboard focus, mobile width, reduced motion, and copy length. Then export the handoff notes.
steps.prompt: Run an interface QA pass on what we built. Check: contrast, focus rings, 375px layout, truncated labels, empty/error copy, reduced-motion. Fix anything broken. End with a short handoff: file list, tokens used, remaining TODOs, and the exact prompt I should reuse next time to extend this UI without drifting the system.

guides.result: A repeatable Quadcode UI workflow — brief, map, system, states, QA — so every new interface starts faster and ships cleaner.

guides.title: Build a SaaS analytics dashboard UI
guides.desc: One brief → dense but readable dashboard: KPI row, chart zone, activity table, and filters that feel native.

steps.title: Frame the dashboard job
steps.text: Name the operator and the decision they make in under 10 seconds. Dashboards without a decision become wallpaper.
steps.prompt: Design a dark SaaS analytics dashboard for a B2B ops lead who needs to spot revenue risk in under 10 seconds. Product: Northline. Sections: left nav, top bar with date range + workspace switcher, KPI strip (4 metrics), main chart, secondary breakdown, and a filterable activity table. Aesthetic: calm, dense, professional — not playful. Stack: single HTML/CSS/JS page.

steps.title: Lay out hierarchy and density
steps.text: KPIs first, chart second, table last. Keep filters sticky. Ask for realistic numbers so the layout is tested with real string lengths.
steps.prompt: Implement the full dashboard layout with realistic sample data (currency, % deltas, timestamps). KPI cards show value + delta + sparkline placeholder. Chart area uses a simple SVG line chart. Table has status pills, search, and column sort affordances. Use CSS grid; no card soup; one clear visual hierarchy.

steps.title: Add interaction without noise
steps.text: Date range, row hover, and empty filter results — enough to feel alive, not a motion demo.
steps.prompt: Add light interactions: date-range segmented control, table filter that updates visible rows, row hover, and an empty-filter state. Prefer CSS transitions under 200ms. Respect prefers-reduced-motion. No purple glow, no glassmorphism.

guides.result: A production-shaped SaaS dashboard UI you can hand to engineering or keep iterating inside Quadcode.

guides.title: Ship a mobile app screen kit
guides.desc: Onboarding, home, and detail — three linked mobile screens with shared components so the product feels like one app.

steps.title: Define the mobile product spine
steps.text: Three screens only for v1: onboarding, home, detail. Everything else waits.
steps.prompt: Plan a mobile consumer app UI kit (390×844). App: Pulse Habit. Screens: (1) onboarding with value prop + CTA, (2) home with today's habits + progress, (3) habit detail with streak and log action. Shared: bottom tab bar, primary button, list row. Light theme, soft neutrals, one accent. Output the screen map first.

steps.title: Generate a visual keyframe for mood
steps.text: One still locks color and photography mood before you code screens — optional but keeps the kit on-brand.
steps.prompt: Clean mobile UI mockup keyframe, habit-tracking home screen on a phone, soft neutral background, coral accent, large readable type, progress rings, calm lifestyle photo header, modern iOS-like spacing, centered product shot, no tiny unreadable text, 9:16

steps.title: Implement the three screens as one system
steps.text: Code them together so tokens and components stay shared. Link with simple in-page navigation.
steps.prompt: Implement all three Pulse Habit screens as one HTML/CSS file with shared tokens and components. Include a simple screen switcher. Use realistic copy. Touch targets ≥44px. Safe-area padding. Empty and completed habit states on home.

guides.result: A coherent three-screen mobile UI kit — onboarding, home, detail — ready to extend or hand off.

guides.title: Atlas Freight — Cinematic Logistics Landing
guides.desc: Build an awwwards-tier dark cinematic freight landing page with an AI-generated hero video, a scroll-driven truck parallax scene and a live route map, using a GPT-Image → Kling → Topaz pipeline.

steps.title: Generate the cinematic hero keyframe
steps.text: Use GPT-Image to create a photorealistic 16:9 aerial keyframe of a container seaport at golden hour. This still becomes the mobile poster and the source frame for the hero video.
steps.prompt: Cinematic aerial hero plate, sprawling container seaport at golden hour, stacked containers in rows, ship-to-shore gantry cranes, container ship at berth, atmospheric haze + low sun flare, warm amber raking light, teal-and-orange cinematic grade, high aerial drone view, strong leading lines, 24mm, deep DOF, photorealistic, film grain. No text/logos/people.

steps.title: Generate the night-loading interlude keyframe
steps.text: A second GPT-Image keyframe — a moody night loading scene — drives a mid-page cinematic interlude with multi-layer parallax.
steps.prompt: Cinematic night plate, containers loaded onto a cargo ship, gantry crane silhouettes vs deep blue-black sky, spreader lifting a container, floodlights + sodium lamps, harbor-light bokeh, industrial fog, moody teal-and-amber low-key grade, wide frame, crane silhouette foreground-left, dark negative space upper for overlay, 35mm, shallow DOF. No text/logos.

steps.title: Generate service card photography
steps.text: Four square GPT-Image stills — ocean, air, road & rail, warehouse — feed the services grid. Keep the same teal-and-amber grade and leave deep shadow area for text legibility.
steps.prompt: Low-angle photoreal container ship at sea, teal-and-amber cinematic grade, deep shadow area lower third for text overlay, no text/logos. (Repeat with matching grade for: cargo freighter loading at sunset / night truck with light trails beside a parallel train / automated high-bay warehouse with AGVs.)

steps.title: Animate the hero keyframe into video
steps.text: Feed the hero keyframe into Kling image-to-video (5s, pro) with slow, steady camera language — no cuts, no shake — so it loops seamlessly behind the fold.
steps.prompt: Very slow dolly-in / Ken Burns push, drifting haze, sun flare, water shimmer, distant crane trolley inches along, extremely smooth steady drone motion, 24mm, deep DOF, single continuous shot. Negative: text, watermark, fast motion, camera shake, cuts, distortion.

steps.title: Animate the interlude keyframe into video
steps.text: Same Kling pipeline on the night-loading keyframe, tuned for a slow lateral drift so it reads well under a 3-layer ScrollTrigger parallax.
steps.prompt: Spreader slowly lowers a container, slow lateral parallax drift, floodlights, twinkling harbor bokeh, rolling fog, rim light on steel, smooth slow motion, single shot.

steps.title: Upscale both clips
steps.text: Run both Kling clips through Topaz video_upscale (Proteus model, 2x, forced H.264) for crisp full-screen playback and guaranteed <video> compatibility.

steps.title: Generate the road-journey parallax assets
steps.text: For the scroll-driven 'trucks physically drive forward' scene, generate the truck and roadside layers with GPT-Image on a flat white background (transparent background isn't supported directly), then key out the white and auto-crop.
steps.prompt: Photoreal semi-truck with container, side profile facing right, graphite-blue metallic body with reflections, glowing headlight bloom, red taillights, amber brand stripe, real tire tread detail, dusk teal-and-amber grade matching a cinematic seaport scene, on a flat pure white background, no shadow, no text/logos.

steps.title: Wire it all together with GSAP ScrollTrigger
steps.text: Hand-code the page in vanilla HTML/CSS/JS with GSAP + ScrollTrigger + MotionPathPlugin (CDN, no build step): hero Ken-Burns scrub, 3-layer interlude parallax, SVG world-map route movers, scroll-scrubbed truck MotionPath with velocity-driven wheel spin/motion-blur/dust, animated counters, a fake-live tracker/dashboard via setInterval, and prefers-reduced-motion + mobile poster fallback throughout.

steps.title: Preview the finished site
steps.text: Here is the actual page in the browser — a real, scrollable single-page site. The AI clips now live inside it as the hero and interlude backgrounds, while GSAP drives the scroll-through: hero headline, the four-mode services grid, the scroll-driven road parallax scene, the live world route map + tracker, the fake-live dashboard, and the quote CTA. Scroll it top to bottom to see every section animate.

guides.result: A fully working, awwwards-tier cinematic logistics landing page — recorded here as a 12-second scroll-through of the real page in the browser. The AI-generated hero and interlude clips are wired in as section backgrounds, wrapped in a hand-coded GSAP site: a scroll-driven truck parallax scene with living motion (spinning wheels, motion blur, dust, dynamic headlight pool), a real animated world route map, a fake-live tracker and dashboard, and a quote CTA — all a single static page with zero backend.

guides.title: Boss fight: one brief, two AI models
guides.desc: Give the same boss-fight brief to two video models and cut the takes side by side — the split-screen format that starts arguments in the comments.

steps.title: Write one brief both models get
steps.text: The comparison only works if the brief is identical. Describe the fight beats — approach, dodge, counter — but leave the world open, so each model shows its own taste in setting and monster design.
steps.prompt: Cinematic dark-fantasy boss fight, game-cinematic quality: a lone armored hero faces a towering monstrous boss, the hero approaches weapon drawn, dodges a massive strike, and counters. Dynamic camera following the action, dramatic lighting, volumetric atmosphere, photoreal detail, 18 seconds

steps.title: Generate a take on each model
steps.text: Run the exact same prompt on both models. In this run one staged a plague abomination in a flooded gothic cathedral, the other a horned demon in a torch-lit colosseum — same beats, totally different directors.

steps.title: Cut the split-screen with labels
steps.text: Place the takes side by side, label each half with the model name, and let them run in sync. Viewers pick a winner themselves — that's the engagement hook. Play the clip above to see the result.

guides.result: An 18-second labeled split-screen where two AI models direct the same boss fight — a format built for 'left or right?' comment wars.

guides.title: Shoot a movie-grade mutation VFX scene
guides.desc: A syringe of glowing serum, a burst of pink energy, and a crystalline monster towering over fleeing scientists — one continuous lab-horror shot.

steps.title: Open on a macro detail
steps.text: Start tight and quiet: a needle piercing a specimen fills the frame. A macro close-up buys believability before the chaos — viewers lean in before the scene turns.
steps.prompt: Extreme macro close-up in a research lab: a syringe filled with glowing neon-pink serum slowly injects into a small yellow gelatinous specimen on a teal lab table, clinical lighting, shallow depth of field, cinematic film look

steps.title: Detonate the transformation
steps.text: Describe the energy burst as a physical event — light, particles, shockwave shaking the room. The camera should get 'hit' by it too: motion blur and grain sell that something went very wrong.
steps.prompt: The specimen erupts in a blinding surge of pink energy, particles and plasma flooding the laboratory, equipment silhouetted against the blast, handheld camera shaken by the shockwave, heavy motion blur and film grain, VFX-heavy cinematic sequence

steps.title: Reveal the creature
steps.text: End wide: the monster standing in the wrecked lab while people run. Naming 'scientists fleeing in panic' gives the creature scale and turns a VFX test into a movie scene. Play the clip above to see the result.
steps.prompt: A towering crystalline monster with glowing pink translucent skin stands in the wrecked laboratory, smoke and pink mist around it, scientists in lab coats fleeing in panic, wide cinematic shot, dramatic overhead lighting, photoreal creature VFX

guides.result: A 10-second lab-horror VFX sequence — macro injection, energy blast, creature reveal — that reads like a blockbuster teaser.

guides.title: Drowned king colossus: two models, one legend
guides.desc: A viking longship meets a crowned colossus rising from the sea — the same epic prompt rendered by two AI models, cut into one split-screen.

steps.title: Script the scene in three beats
steps.text: Big prompts fall apart without structure. Lock three beats — the colossus looms over the ship, the ship capsizes underwater, an extreme close-up of the giant's eye — and both models have to hit the same marks.
steps.prompt: Epic dark-fantasy sequence in three beats: 1) a viking longship with shields and torn sail sails toward a colossal drowned king — a mountain-sized ancient giant with a crown, seaweed beard and waterfalls pouring off his stone-like skin, storm sky; 2) underwater shot of the capsized longship sinking, vikings and shields drifting down in green murky water; 3) extreme close-up of the colossus' enormous pale eye opening underwater. Cinematic photoreal detail, 18 seconds

steps.title: Run it on both models
steps.text: Same prompt, two models, no cherry-picking edits inside a take. The differences you keep — crown design, water physics, how the eye opens — are exactly what makes the comparison worth watching.

steps.title: Sync the halves and label them
steps.text: Line both takes up beat-for-beat in a split-screen and put the model names on top. Matching beats side by side is what makes quality differences impossible to miss. Play the clip above to see the result.

guides.result: An 18-second split-screen epic where two AI models raise the same drowned king from the sea — beat-synced so the differences do the talking.

guides.title: Fake a viral walking-on-water clip
guides.desc: A woman steps off the pier and runs across the ocean while the crowd films — the impossible moment that floods comment sections.

steps.title: Stage it like real phone footage
steps.text: The trick is the witnesses. Put a crowd with phones on the pier — if bystanders are filming, viewers' brains read the clip as documentary, not CGI.
steps.prompt: Handheld phone footage at a tropical resort pier, a crowd of tourists in swimwear stands on the wooden dock filming with their phones, crystal-clear turquoise lagoon, a small boat anchored in the distance, bright midday sun, candid vertical-video energy, realistic colors, no cinematic grading

steps.title: Direct the impossible moment
steps.text: Describe one continuous action with real physics: she steps off the pier onto the water and runs. Name the splashes and the foam trail — believable water reaction is what sells the fake.
steps.prompt: A young woman in a white crop top and black shorts steps off the pier directly onto the ocean surface and walks on the water, then breaks into a run across the lagoon toward the beach, her feet slap the surface leaving splashes and a long white foam trail behind her, the crowd on the pier keeps filming in disbelief, camera follows her from behind, natural body mechanics, realistic water physics, 11 seconds

steps.title: Keep the take that fools people
steps.text: Generate a few takes and keep the one where the feet actually read as striking the surface — one bad footstep breaks the whole illusion. Play the clip above to see the result.

guides.result: An 11-second walking-on-water clip that looks like real bystander footage — built to stop the scroll and start the 'how is this possible' comments.

guides.title: Make a screen-to-reality hero video
guides.desc: A parrot flies out of a laptop screen and lands on your hand — the wow-moment hero clip that stops visitors cold.

steps.title: Stage the screen inside the scene
steps.text: Generate one keyframe where the laptop screen shows the exact subject that will break out of it. Matching light between screen and scene is what sells the trick later.
steps.prompt: Photorealistic shot of an open silver laptop on a dark wooden table on a tropical veranda at golden hour, the screen displays a vivid scarlet macaw parrot filling the frame, lush palm jungle bokeh behind the railing, warm low sun flaring through the trees, screen glow matching the ambient light, centered composition, 16:9

steps.title: Direct the breakout moment
steps.text: Describe one continuous action: the subject leaves the screen and enters the real scene. Name the landing point — a hand entering the frame gives the camera somewhere to go.
steps.prompt: The scarlet macaw on the laptop screen comes alive and flies out of the screen into the real scene, wings spreading wide toward the camera, feathers catching the golden sunlight, then it gently lands on a person's outstretched hand in the foreground, camera pulls back smoothly to follow the flight, photorealistic motion, natural wing physics, seamless transition from screen to reality, 10 seconds

steps.title: Ship it as your landing hero
steps.text: Keep the take where the screen-exit reads clean and the landing feels weighty. Drop it into your page hero with autoplay, mute and loop — the first three seconds do all the selling. Play the clip above to see the result.

guides.result: A 10-second screen-to-reality hero video in 4K — the kind of opening shot that makes visitors stop scrolling and watch your landing page.

guides.title: Spin a 360° product hero for your landing page
guides.desc: One product photo becomes a seamless 4K turntable loop — the hero video that makes a landing page feel expensive.

steps.title: Generate the hero keyframe
steps.text: Start with one strong product shot — this single image locks the look of the whole loop. Match it to your brand palette so the video drops straight into your page.
steps.prompt: Photorealistic product shot of a frosted glass cosmetic jar labeled SAKURA GLOW CREAM 50ml with a rose-gold metallic lid, standing on a dark glossy reflective surface, soft cherry blossom petals drifting in the air and resting nearby, warm dusty-rose studio backdrop, premium beauty ad lighting, centered composition, 16:9

steps.title: Turn it into a seamless 360° loop
steps.text: Feed the same image as both the start and end frame with transition mode on — that is what makes the loop invisible. Describe only the rotation, nothing else.
steps.prompt: The jar rotates slowly through a full 360° turntable spin on the glossy surface, camera locked, cherry blossom petals drifting gently in the background, soft studio reflections gliding across the rose-gold lid, smooth constant speed, seamless loop — first and last frame identical

steps.title: Upscale to 4K and ship it
steps.text: Run the finished loop through the upscaler for a crisp 4K master, then drop it into your landing page hero with autoplay, mute and loop. Play the clip above to see the result.

guides.result: A seamless 16-second 360° product turntable in 4K — the hero video that turns a static landing page into a premium storefront.

guides.title: Build a luxury beauty brand landing page
guides.desc: Three prompts turn into a polished, scroll-ready skincare landing page — the agent writes all the code for you.

steps.title: Set the brand and mood
steps.text: Give the agent one clear brief: brand name, product, aesthetic and audience. Name a real design direction — 'Japanese minimalism' works harder than 'make it pretty'.
steps.prompt: Build a single-page landing site for BLOOM CARE — a premium Japanese skincare brand built around sakura extract. Aesthetic: Japanese minimalism, soft blush-pink and cream palette, elegant serif headlines, generous whitespace. The page should feel like a quiet luxury boutique, calm and editorial, not a loud e-commerce store.

steps.title: Structure the page
steps.text: List the sections in order so the page tells one story: hook, products, hero product, proof, full collection. The agent builds every section with real layout and copy.
steps.prompt: Page structure, top to bottom: (1) sticky header with logo, nav and cart; (2) hero — headline "The art of timeless beauty", one-line subcopy, two buttons, product photo on the right; (3) bestsellers row — three product cards with prices and add-to-cart; (4) hero product spotlight — Sakura Repair Serum with key actives listed in a spec table; (5) formulation story — cherry blossom imagery with short claims; (6) full collection grid. Write all copy in polished brand English.

steps.title: Polish and record
steps.text: Finish with the details that sell the premium feel: micro-animations, a benefits marquee, hover states. Then scroll the live page and screen-record it for your portfolio or client.
steps.prompt: Add finishing touches: a slim scrolling marquee strip with offers (free samples, cruelty-free, 30-day guarantee, free shipping), subtle fade-up animations on scroll, soft hover lift on product cards, and a typing caret effect on the hero headline. Keep every animation quiet and smooth — premium, never flashy.

guides.result: A complete luxury skincare landing page coded by the agent — recorded here as a 12-second scroll-through, ready to ship or hand to a client.

guides.title: Create your own RPG character
guides.desc: Turn one idea into a unique, animated 3D RPG hero you can spin around and drop into your game.

steps.title: Describe your hero
steps.text: Write one short line about who your character is: their class, vibe and one signature detail. Keep it simple and specific.
steps.prompt: A mythical jade sorcerer, ancient guardian, standing hero pose, ornate carved gold armor over deep emerald jade scales, dragon-like crest helmet, glowing arcane engravings, fantasy RPG game character, stylized realism, full body

steps.title: Lock the look and details
steps.text: Add color, material and mood so the character feels one-of-a-kind, not generic. Name real materials so it reads as game-ready art.
steps.prompt: Polished emerald jade, weathered antique gold filigree, intricate ancient runes, subtle green subsurface glow, moody teal underground lighting, clean readable silhouette, relaxed hero stance on a round base, single character

steps.title: Turn it into a 3D model
steps.text: Send the image to the 3D step to get a real, rotatable mesh with clean topology and baked textures.
steps.prompt: Convert this character into a 3D model: full body, clean topology, textured, game-ready, T-pose friendly, consistent proportions, PBR materials

steps.title: Spin, check and export
steps.text: Rotate the model to check it from every side, fix anything off, then export. Play the clip above to see the final turntable.

guides.result: You now have a unique, fully rotatable 3D RPG character, ready to animate and drop straight into your game.

guides.title: Make a game crossover poster
guides.desc: Mix two games into one bold comic-style banner with a single prompt.

steps.title: One prompt, whole poster
steps.text: Describe the scene, both characters, the style and the text in a single prompt. Run it a few times and keep the version with the cleanest lettering.
steps.prompt: Wide horizontal comic-book crossover poster mixing two games. Upper-left panel: a bald assassin in a sharp black suit and red tie kneels on a rooftop, aiming a long sniper rifle across the skyline, covering the hero below. Lower-right panel: a young man in a white tank top with a tattooed arm walks a sun-drenched neon city street at dusk holding a pistol, palm trees, retro convertible cars, pink and orange sunset. The two panels are split by one bold diagonal comic slash so it reads as protection, not an ambush. Bold ink outlines, halftone shading, saturated neon colors. Big comic logo at the top combining both game names, and a caption box at the bottom reading "ESPECIALLY DANGEROUS MISSION". Keep all text crisp and correctly spelled, no watermarks.

guides.result: A sharp, comic-style crossover banner ready to post or use as cover art.

guides.title: Make a jazz R&B restaurant track
guides.desc: Score a smooth, upscale fine-dining lounge track with one prompt.

steps.title: Set the vibe
steps.text: Say the mood and place in one line. For a premium restaurant, keep it smooth and classy.
steps.prompt: Sophisticated jazz R&B instrumental for an upscale fine-dining restaurant lounge, smooth, elegant and relaxed

steps.title: Name the instruments
steps.text: List a few real instruments so it sounds live and warm, not synthetic.
steps.prompt: Warm Rhodes electric piano, soft brushed drums, walking upright bass, mellow muted trumpet and tenor saxophone, lush jazz chords

steps.title: Set tempo and finish
steps.text: Add a slow tempo and keep it instrumental so it never gets in the way of conversation.
steps.prompt: Relaxed 85 BPM, subtle vinyl warmth, smooth dynamics, tasteful and understated, no vocals

steps.title: Generate and pick your take
steps.text: Run it, listen, and keep the version that feels the most refined. Press play below to hear the result.

guides.result: A polished jazz R&B instrumental ready to loop in a restaurant, cafe, or lounge.

guides.title: Shoot a 2000s camcorder memory
guides.desc: Turn one prompt into a nostalgic MiniDV street clip that feels like real found footage.

steps.title: Pick the camera, not the style
steps.text: The trick is to describe the device that filmed it. Name a real 2000s camcorder look and the AI stops making glossy footage.
steps.prompt: Footage filmed on a 2004 MiniDV handycam with a fisheye lens attachment, slight barrel distortion, soft focus, washed-out colors, mild tape noise, white date-time stamp in the corner reading 2004. 5.29 AM 10:47, 4:3 home-video framing

steps.title: Direct one small, real moment
steps.text: Big scenes look fake on old tape. One quiet everyday moment sells the illusion — say exactly who does what.
steps.prompt: A quiet back alley in Seoul on a sunny morning, a young woman with a loose ponytail crouches on the cracked pavement and slowly reaches her open hand toward a stray tabby cat, the cat sniffs her fingers, old tires and a dark green metal gate behind them, candid handheld moment

steps.title: Generate and keep the honest take
steps.text: Run both parts as one prompt in Seedance, 15 seconds. Pick the take with natural motion — small pauses and imperfect framing are what make it believable. Play the clip above to see the result.

guides.result: A 15-second retro camcorder clip that looks like a real memory from 2004 — ready for reels, moodboards or a music video.

guides.title: Mass-produce UGC ads from one product photo
guides.desc: Build an AI actor, hand them your product and generate ultra-realistic UGC ad clips on repeat.

steps.title: Create your AI actor
steps.text: Generate one consistent, believable creator with GPT-Image. Keep the look casual and trustworthy — this face will front all your ads.
steps.prompt: Photorealistic portrait of a female UGC creator in her early 30s, natural blonde hair, soft daylight, beige wool coat over a cream turtleneck, neutral warm background, looking at camera with a calm friendly expression, shot on a mirrorless camera, 85mm, realistic skin texture, no retouching look

steps.title: Put the product in her hands
steps.text: Combine the actor and your product photo into one natural shot. Name the product and the action — the AI does the compositing.
steps.prompt: The same woman naturally holds and presents a black leather tote bag to the camera, fingers resting on the top edge of the bag, soft indoor daylight, candid UGC style, product clearly visible and in focus, realistic hands, no studio look

steps.title: Bring it to life with video
steps.text: Send the composite shot to Kling and describe one simple creator movement. Short, natural motion sells it — repeat with new angles for endless variations.
steps.prompt: The UGC creator presents and shows the bag to camera: she lifts it slightly, turns it to show the front, and smiles — natural handheld framing, soft motion, realistic lighting, 5 seconds

guides.result: A repeatable pipeline: one AI actor + one product photo = a stream of ultra-realistic UGC ad clips, ready for TikTok, Reels and paid campaigns.

guides.title: Turn a photo into a living particle poster
guides.desc: One photo becomes thousands of dots that scatter under your cursor — a hero page coded by the agent from three prompts.

steps.title: Turn the photo into dots
steps.text: Ask the agent to build a page where your image is redrawn as a halftone grid — thousands of tiny circles, sized by brightness. Pick a photo with strong contrast on a dark background.
steps.prompt: Build a single-page hero section on a pure black background. Center: a photorealistic seashell image rendered as a halftone particle grid — thousands of small circular dots sampled from the image, dot size driven by pixel brightness. Smooth 60fps canvas rendering, subtle floor reflection under the shell.

steps.title: Make it react to the cursor
steps.text: Add the hook: dots near the cursor fly away with soft physics and drift back when you leave. This one interaction is what makes people stop and play.
steps.prompt: Add interaction: when the cursor moves across the image, nearby dots scatter away with soft spring physics and slowly drift back into place when the cursor leaves. Keep the motion smooth and organic — no jitter, no lag.

steps.title: Frame it like a poster
steps.text: Finish with an editorial typography layer around the artwork so the page reads as a premium poster, not a tech demo. Then record the page as a video to share.
steps.prompt: Add an editorial typography layer: large serif logotype top-left, small uppercase spaced labels — a tagline top-right, a collection label bottom-left, a "MOVE CURSOR TO DISSOLVE" hint bottom-right, and a small date tag rotated on the left edge. Premium editorial poster look, all text crisp.

guides.result: An interactive particle-poster web page that dissolves under the cursor — recorded here as a 13-second clip, ready for your portfolio or socials.

guides.title: Make a premium product montage
guides.desc: Turn one product into a slick, ad-style video with a few simple shots.

steps.title: Plan the film with one image
steps.text: Generate one storyboard sheet with GPT-Image. It locks the look of every shot before you spend a single video credit.
steps.prompt: A professional film storyboard sheet for a 20-second premium headphones spec ad, dark cinematic layout on black. Eight 16:9 frames in a grid, each with a caption and timecode: box reveal under a single spotlight, hero product floating on black with a specular light sweep, macro of the dial and brushed metal, macro of the knit mesh cushion, person wearing the headphones in a neon city at night, night walk on a wet reflective street, brand end card with the product on a glossy floor, final slow push-in. Consistent silver over-ear headphones in every frame, Apple-style product cinematography, clean typography, high-end pitch deck look.

steps.title: Shot 1 — the reveal
steps.text: Feed the storyboard frame into Seedance and generate the opening clip. One box, one light, nothing else.
steps.prompt: A pristine white product box on a dark reflective floor under one soft spotlight. The lid slowly lifts and warm light blooms from inside, revealing silver over-ear headphones nested in the packaging. Slow overhead push-in, quiet and premium, dark studio, no text.

steps.title: Shot 2 — the hero
steps.text: Now the money shot: the product alone on black with light doing all the work.
steps.prompt: Silver over-ear headphones float centered on a pure black background. A single specular light streak sweeps across the aluminium ear cups as the product slowly rotates. Slow drifting camera, high-contrast studio lighting, shallow depth of field, premium ad style, no text.

steps.title: Shot 3 — real life
steps.text: One lifestyle clip so the product lives outside the studio. Keep it moody and cinematic.
steps.prompt: A young man wearing silver over-ear headphones walks away from camera through a neon-lit city street at night, wet asphalt reflecting the signs, cinematic bokeh from car lights, smooth forward tracking shot, moody, immersive and premium.

steps.title: Shot 4 — the end card
steps.text: Close on the brand, then stitch all four clips together in any editor.
steps.prompt: Silver over-ear headphones stand centered on a glossy black floor with a soft mirror reflection. The camera pushes in slowly while the light gently dims to black. Minimal premium brand end-card mood, elegant and calm, no text.

guides.result: A slick, ad-style product montage ready to post or use as a promo video.
